entity.name: SyncLite Project
entity.description: The SyncLite Project develops and maintains Apache 2.0 licensed open-source software for reliable, vendor-neutral edge-to-cloud data synchronization.

projects.name: SyncLite
projects.description: SyncLite is an Apache 2.0 licensed open-source platform for offline-first and edge-to-cloud data synchronization. It enables developers to build reliable, exactly-once data replication pipelines into databases, data warehouses, data lakes, and analytics platforms using open standards and developer-friendly APIs.

funding.description: SyncLite is developed in the open under the Apache 2.0 License. Community funding helps ensure the project remains free, vendor-neutral, well-maintained, and accessible to developers worldwide. Contributions support engineering, testing, documentation, security, CI/CD infrastructure, cross-platform SDKs, connectors, developer experience, and the long-term sustainability of the project.

channels.description: Support the long-term sustainability of the SyncLite Project through GitHub Sponsors.

plans.name: Community Supporter
plans.description: Help support maintenance, bug fixes, documentation, testing, and regular releases.

plans.name: Project Sponsor
plans.description: Help fund new connectors, SDKs, platform compatibility, developer tooling, and continuous integration.

plans.name: Ecosystem Sponsor
plans.description: Support the growth of the SyncLite ecosystem through investments in the Open Replication Format, performance, reliability, security, documentation, community initiatives, and long-term sustainability.
